query.help: Search for items on Namecheap. Example: namecheap search query "my search term" --limit 20 --table namecheap search query "keyword" --filter "price:lt:100" namecheap search query "keyword" --properties "title,price"
query.usage_instructions: Search Namecheap items using a free-text query. Pass the required query text, `--limit/-l` to cap results, `--filter/-f` for client-side filtering, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

item.help: Get details for a specific item. Example: namecheap search item abc123 --table namecheap search item "https://example.com/item/abc123" namecheap search item abc123 --properties "title,price"
item.usage_instructions: Get one Namecheap item by ID or URL. Pass the required item identifier, add `--table/-t` for a key-value table, and use `--properties/-p` to limit the returned fields.

list.help: List items from Namecheap. Example: namecheap search list --limit 100 --table namecheap search list --filter "status:active" namecheap search list --properties "id,name,status"
list.usage_instructions: List Namecheap items from the scaffolded browser source. Use `--limit/-l` to cap items, `--filter/-f` for client-side filtering, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

wildcard.help: Search items with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Browser CLIs use client-side search.
wildcard.usage_instructions: Run client-side wildcard matching against scaffolded Namecheap items. Pass a required wildcard query, use `--fields` to scope matching, `--limit/-l` to cap the scan, `--table/-t` for tabular output, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

search.usage_instructions: Generic scaffold search surface for Namecheap. Use these commands to query, list, inspect, or wildcard-match items once the browser parser and client methods are implemented for the target site.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Open or refresh the saved browser session for Namecheap. Use `--profile/-p` for a named profile, `--force/-F` to clear and re-authenticate, and `--credential-type/-c` only when you need to target one credential type.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear saved `namecheap` credentials and browser session data. Use `--profile/-p` to remove one profile instead of all saved sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check whether saved Namecheap credentials are present and authenticated. Use `--profile/-p` to inspect one profile or `--table/-t` for a compact multi-profile view.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Refresh OAuth credentials if this tool later adds them. Use `--profile/-p` to target one profile and `--table/-t` when you want the result in tabular form.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Verify that the saved browser session for Namecheap is still usable. Use `--profile/-p` to scope the check, `--table/-t` for summary output, and `--verbose/-v` for per-check details.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List saved `namecheap` authentication profiles. Use `--table/-t` for tabular output, `--limit/-l` to cap the result set, `--filter/-f` to narrow profiles, and `--properties/-p` to select fields.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show details for one saved `namecheap` profile. Pass the profile name as the required argument and add `--table/-t` for a key-value table view.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a named `namecheap` profile from the template config. Use when you need a separate saved session for another Namecheap account or environment.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Mark one `namecheap` profile as the default profile. Use when future auth and data commands should use that profile unless `--profile/-p` is passed explicitly.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named `namecheap` profile and its stored data. Use when a saved session is no longer needed or must be recreated cleanly.

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named authentication profiles for `namecheap`. Use these commands when you need separate saved sessions for different Namecheap accounts or environments.

auth.usage_instructions: Manage browser-session authentication for Namecheap. Use `auth login` to create or refresh a session, `auth status` or `auth test` to verify it, and `auth profiles ...` to manage named saved sessions.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Delete cached `namecheap` responses for the active profile. Use this when you need fresh results instead of cached browser-authenticated reads.

cache.usage_instructions: Inspect or clear cached `namecheap` responses. Use cache commands when you need to confirm cache state or force fresh browser-authenticated reads.

usage_instructions: Use for browser-based Namecheap operations through the `namecheap` CLI. Commands follow `namecheap <group> <action>`; run `namecheap auth login` first, use `auth status` or `auth test` to verify the saved session, and use `search` commands after the site-specific parser is implemented. Pass `--no-cache` when you need an uncached browser-authenticated read.
